programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Having committed over 27 assaults on correctional officers, Billy Tracy is one of the most violent offenders on death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In October 2004, Rachel O'Reilly left her home to drop her children off at school. Less than 20 minutes after returning home she was murdered. Her husband, Joe O'Reilly, staged the crime scene to make it look like a burglary gone wrong.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of Richard Roszkowski, the Connecticut triple-murderer who killed his ex-girlfriend, her nine-year-old daughter and another man in a brutal attack in 2006.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The 'housemate from hell' who plots the death of his two elderly landlords in a bid to inherit from their estates.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British Police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Over six years, Eddie Seda, the copycat Zodiac killer stalked the streets of New York City, in search of easy targets. His cowardly attacks culminated with the ferocious murder of a woman, who had only asked him for a cigarette.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Stephen Farrow committed two murders in early 2012 motivated by an obsessive hatred of Christianity, which he attributed to sexual abuse from a priest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2003, Kenneth Regan and William Horncy murdered a millionaire and his young family. The killers wiped out three generations of the Chohan family, including two young children.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Carol Jarvis disappeared, her husband told friends and family that she'd simply gone on a little holiday.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Lee Ford, who killed his wife and four stepchildren in a cold-blooded murder spree over 24 hours in September 2000.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: She became the first woman to be executed by lethal injection after taking six lives. What drove her to commit these callous murders?

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Sent to prison after the torture and violent murder of a mother of six, Jamie Osuna is now awaiting trial.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A married couple decided to escape the cold of winter with a mini-vacation in Key West. The wife went missing, and police searched every square inch of the island, they found nothing but a pair of sandals which might have belonged to her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home in Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A beautiful young woman falls to her death from a cliff overlooking the ocean. Initially, investigators think it was a tragic accident. But after a forensic examination, police uncover a very different picture of what happened.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body. Finally, investigators use a single tooth found among some charred remains to identify her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young couple decided to celebrate their first wedding anniversary with a camping trip to Mount Hood. During the trip, the husband was shot and killed. Later, his wife told two different versions of the events which led to his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young couple decided to celebrate their first wedding anniversary with a camping trip to Mount Hood. During the trip, the husband was shot and killed. Later, his wife told two different versions of the events which led to his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman convicted of killing her mother in a car fire is granted a new trial. An expert witness has a different opinion as to how the fire started and, if proven, would clear the woman of any culpability.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: No one in a residential community saw anything the day one of their neighbors was brutally murdered. Fingerprints found at the crime scene helped investigators to apprehend the presumed killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a trio of unsolved murders occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas. Later, a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with far more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A three-year-old develops a respiratory condition. His father has Alzheimers. An investigator reveals that their ill health is due to mold in their modern home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman disappeared without a trace, there were two possible explanations: kidnapping or murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating an apparent overdose suicide, detectives recover incriminating files from a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1962, the people of the small town of Hanford, California lost their sense of peace when one of their own, 15-year-old Marlene Miller, was murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Just weeks before a witness is to testify against the man accused of sexually assaulting her, she is murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bombings are difficult to solve, because the perpetrator isn't usually at the scene, and the evidence goes up in smoke. In this case pieces of plastic the size of grains of sand hold the key to a man's murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, a high school girl vanished after attending a party. The prime suspect had two alibi witnesses for the night she disappeared. A solid alibi can often overcome circumstantial evidence, but forensic evidence is another matter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teen was killed by her parents in what they said was self-defense, but forensic evidence told otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A California teen went missing. Police suspected she'd run away until her body was discovered in a ditch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man is found dead in his home, and his ex-wife has a perfect alibi. To determine time of death, investigators need to know when the victim ate his last meal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead from a gunshot wound and police suspect her husband, however, forensic teams prove what really happened and the truth is stranger than fiction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A go-go dancer told her family she needed to get away for a while and then she simply disappeared. Investigators were suspicious, because she took neither her car nor her clothes. 20 years later, her family received a call that changed everything.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A coroner finds telltale clues on the badly burned body of a murder victim, sending police in pursuit of a couple of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A nurse experiences a variety of flu-like symptoms. None of her doctors are able to discover the cause, until she visits the gynecologist for a routine check-up.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a discarded sleeping bag containing bloody sneakers and a purse, which eventually leads to the body of a young woman. A mark found on the victim's body enables police to track the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are led on a wild goose chase when the wife of a prominent banker is kidnapped. An entomologist's unexpected discovery leads to a new suspect - and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A neighbor watched as a man stabbed his wife, pushed her into the swimming pool and held her head under water.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A cop is found dead. His wife first says the motive was robbery; then she tells police it was suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people witness a truck driving over an unconscious man and flee into a nearby bar, but when the police turn up, the truck, the man and the witnesses are gone.

programme.title: That '80s Show
programme.desc: A group of 20-somethings navigates through San Diego in the midst of the trends and politics of the 1980s. Stereotypically, the punks, the material girls, the yuppies and the Madonna wannabes all hang together and find reasons to frequent a record store run by a former groupie. Regular characters on the show include struggling musician Corey Howard, punk-rocker June Tuesday and Corey's father, R.T., who is representative of the decade's mentality of buying oneself extravagant gifts and having an excessive lifestyle.
